channel.title: CyberNetSec - Policy and Compliance
channel.description: Latest policy and compliance threat intelligence - the last 10 articles

item.title: EU Unveils 'CYBER-AI Act' with Strict Security Mandates and Heavy Fines for High-Risk AI
item.description:
The European Commission has introduced the 'Cybersecurity of Artificial Intelligence Act' (CYBER-AI Act), a new regulation that imposes stringent cybersecurity requirements on high-risk AI systems. Working alongside the existing AI Act, this legislation mandates secure-by-design principles, threat m...

Severity: Informational

Categories: Policy and Compliance, Regulatory, Threat Intelligence

Read Full Article →

item.title: AI-Powered Disinformation is Top Threat to 2028 Elections, Mandiant Warns
item.description:
A new report from Mandiant (part of Google Cloud) identifies AI-powered disinformation and influence operations as the most significant cyber threat to the 2028 U.S. election cycle. The report warns of a future filled with hyper-realistic deepfakes and AI-driven social media manipulation designed to...

Severity: Informational

Categories: Threat Intelligence, Policy and Compliance, Other

Read Full Article →

item.title: EDPB Adopts Common Template for GDPR Data Breach Notifications
item.description:
The European Data Protection Board (EDPB) has adopted a common, standardized template for organizations to use when notifying supervisory authorities of a personal data breach under the GDPR. This initiative aims to simplify the compliance process and harmonize the currently fragmented reporting req...

Severity: Informational

Categories: Policy and Compliance, Regulatory, Data Breach

Read Full Article →

item.title: TUANZ Calls for "Security by Design" in New Zealand's Digital Future
item.description:
The Tech Users Association of New Zealand (TUANZ) has published a new position paper urging a fundamental shift in the nation's cybersecurity strategy. The group is calling for a move away from the current 'user beware' model, which places a heavy burden on individuals and SMEs. Instead, TUANZ advoc...

Severity: Informational

Categories: Policy and Compliance, Security Operations

Read Full Article →

item.title: Accenture and ServiceNow Unveil Joint AI Offering to Modernize Enterprise Risk Management
item.description:
Accenture and ServiceNow have launched a joint offering that combines AI-powered managed security services with an automated migration solution. The platform aims to help organizations move from legacy risk systems to a modern, unified platform, enabling them to combat the rising speed and sophistic...

Severity: Informational

Categories: Security Operations, Policy and Compliance, Cloud Security

Read Full Article →

item.title: Redwood AI Enters Post-Quantum Cryptography Market with Acquisition of Quantum.IQ
item.description:
Redwood AI Corp. has entered into a definitive agreement to acquire Quantum.IQ Technologies, a Vancouver-based company specializing in AI-driven, post-quantum cryptography (PQC) solutions. The move marks Redwood AI's strategic expansion from its focus on artificial intelligence into the emerging and...

Severity: Informational

Categories: Policy and Compliance, Threat Intelligence, Other

Read Full Article →

item.title: Accenture Bets $4.175B on OT Security, Acquiring Dragos, runZero, and NetRise
item.description:
Accenture is making a monumental $4.175 billion investment to secure a leading position in the operational technology (OT) security market. The consulting giant is acquiring a majority stake in OT threat intelligence leader Dragos, while fully acquiring asset discovery firm runZero and firmware secu...

Severity: Informational

Categories: Industrial Control Systems, Policy and Compliance, Threat Intelligence

Read Full Article →

item.title: Linux Foundation Launches 'Akrites' Framework to Secure Open Source from AI Threats
item.description:
The Linux Foundation, along with major tech companies like Google, Microsoft, and OpenAI, has launched 'Akrites,' a new cross-industry security framework. The initiative aims to create a unified process for vulnerability disclosure and remediation in critical open-source software. This move is a dir...

Severity: Informational

Categories: Policy and Compliance, Security Operations, Supply Chain Attack

Read Full Article →

item.title: FCC Adopts New Cybersecurity Rules to Safeguard Emergency Alert System (EAS)
item.description:
On June 25, 2026, the U.S. Federal Communications Commission (FCC) adopted new rules requiring operators of the Emergency Alert System (EAS) to implement specific cybersecurity measures to protect the critical public warning infrastructure. The rules mandate actions like installing firewalls, monito...

Severity: Informational

Categories: Policy and Compliance, Regulatory, Industrial Control Systems

Read Full Article →

item.title: CISA Shifts Strategy to Protect 'Crown Jewels' of Critical Infrastructure
item.description:
The U.S. Cybersecurity and Infrastructure Security Agency (CISA) is shifting its strategy from a broad, sector-based approach to one that focuses on identifying and protecting the specific assets and systems—the 'crown jewels'—most critical to national security and public safety. Acting Director Nic...

Severity: Informational

Categories: Policy and Compliance, Regulatory, Industrial Control Systems

Read Full Article →
